title: Do Auctioneers Pick Optimal Reserve Prices?

design.mechanism: Second-price sealed-bid auction (described as English auction)
design.bidder_type: Computerized bidders following weakly dominant strategy (bid equal to valuation)

steps: Seller receives complete auction instructions including rules, profit calculation examples, and a table and histogram of the drop-out price distribution.
Seller is assigned a demographic profile (age, gender, race, academic program).
Each round, seller observes the number of computerized bidders (randomly drawn from 1, 4, 7, or 10, equally likely).
Computerized bidders bid up to their private valuations (drawn from the Cuberoot distribution). Bidders with valuations below the reserve price do not participate.
Seller receives feedback: drop-out prices for bidders above the reserve, whether some bidders were below the reserve, the winning bid, and profit for the round.
Seller sees cumulative history of all previous rounds before making the next decision.
